items.title: The Secret Life of Python: The Loophole
items.author: Aaron Rose
items.content:
The trap of modifying a list while iterating over it (and how to fix it).

The library was warm, but Timothy was sweating. He was staring at a list of sensor data on his screen.

"I don't understand," he muttered. "I told it to remove the errors. I wrote the code explicitly. But it keeps missing them."

Margaret glided over, placing a fresh pot of tea on the table. "Missing them?"

"Yes," Timothy said. "I have a list of temperature readings. Any reading below zero is an error—a glitch. I wrote a loop to find them and delete them."

"Watch," Timothy said. "I have two negative numbers: and . The loop should catch both."

Removing glitch: -5
Cleaned List: [10, -2, 15, 20]

Timothy threw his hands up. "It missed the ! It looked right at it and walked on by. Is the loop broken?"

"The loop is not broken," Margaret said calmly. "The floor is moving."

Margaret pulled a few books off the shelf and laid them in a row on the table.[Book A] [Book B] [Book C] [Book D]

"Imagine these are your list items," she said. "The loop uses an internal pointer—an index—to track where it is. It starts at Index 0."

She pointed to the first book. "Step 0." Then she moved her finger. "Step 1: The negative number."

"Now," Margaret said, "you tell Python to the item at Step 1." She pulled out of the line.

Immediately, she slid the remaining books to the left to fill the gap.[Book A] [Book C] [Book D]

"What just happened?" she asked.

"Everything shifted left," Timothy said.

"Exactly. The item that at Index 2 () has now slid into Index 1. But the loop doesn't know the floor moved. The loop just finishes Step 1 and says, 'Okay, time for Step 2'."

She moved her finger to the Index 2 ().

"See?" Margaret whispered. "You skipped . Because you destroyed the step you were standing on, the next step fell into the hole, and you stepped right over it."

"That's why it missed the !" Timothy realized. "It slid into the slot I just cleared, and the loop jumped past it."

"So I can never modify a list loop?" Timothy asked.

"You can," Margaret said. "But you must not modify the list you are walking on. You must walk on a map, and modify the territory."

She deleted his loop and typed a subtle change.

Cleaned List: [10, 15, 20]

"By adding ," Margaret explained, "you iterate over a of the list—the same trick we used to avoid aliasing, but here it serves a different purpose. The loop walks safely on the copy—which never changes—while you delete items from the original list. The indices on your copy never shift, so you never skip a beat."

"Is there a better way?" Timothy asked. "Removing items one by one feels... slow."

"There is," Margaret smiled. "Instead of fixing a broken world, simply build a new one."

She showed him the approach.

"This is often faster and cleaner," she said. "We don't modify. We just filter."

Margaret opened her notebook to the "Loops" section, drawing a warning sign.

  • Never add or remove items from a list you are looping over it.
  • Skipped items (if removing) or infinite loops (if adding).
  • When you change the list length, the indices shift, but the loop counter keeps marching forward.
  • (Quick fix).
  • [x for x in my_list if condition] (Best for filtering).
  • for item in reversed(my_list): (Advanced—useful when you modify in-place without copying).

Timothy looked at his clean data. "I feel like I was trying to change a tire while the car was moving."

"Worse," Margaret replied, pouring the tea. "You were trying to remove the floorboards while dancing on them."

In the next episode, Margaret and Timothy will face "The Lazy Baker"—where they discover that some "lists" aren't lists at all, but lazy machines (generators) waiting to produce data on demand.

items.title: Building Blackjack for the Terminal: My First Python Project
items.author: Jonathan Key
items.content:
For my latest programming assignment from Codecademy, I decided to build a game. I chose this project because I wanted to practice translating real-world rules (like the complex logic of an Ace being either 1 or 11) into clean, functional Python code.

My goal was to create a game that felt interactive and "smart" enough to play against a dealer, all within a simple text-based interface.

Here is a look at the gameplay loop, showing the deck shuffling, the deal, and the logic behind hitting and standing:

This project was a great way to dive deeper into Python's core features. Here are a few technical highlights from the build:

  • I used a to map card ranks (like 'J', 'Q', 'K') to their numeric values. This made it easy to efficiently calculate hand totals.
  • To ensure every game is different, I used Python’s module. This replicates a real deck being mixed before it is dealt.
  • The core of the game is a loop that keeps the "Hit or Stand" phase active until the player either stops or "busts" (goes over 21).
  • One of the trickiest parts was the Ace logic. I implemented logic to check whether a hand exceeds 21 and automatically adjust an Ace's value from 11 to 1 if necessary.

Check Out the Source Code

You can explore the full logic, including how I handled the dealer's AI and the game flow, on my GitHub:

Building this game taught me that the hardest part of coding isn't just writing syntax, it's planning for every possible user decision. Seeing the logic come together to successfully crown a winner (or bust a player!) was incredibly satisfying.

I'm looking forward to adding more features, like a or a to support multiple decks, in the future!

items.title: Sensor Fusion Explained: How to Build a High-Accuracy Fitness Rep Counter
items.author: wellallyTech
items.content:
Fitness apps are everywhere, yet many fail at one fundamental task: accurately counting repetitions for complex movements. If you have ever seen an app count "phantom" reps or miss a set entirely, you have seen the limits of single-sensor tracking.

The accelerometers in our phones are naturally , while gyroscopes suffer from over time. To solve this, developers use "sensor fusion" to create a stable, reliable signal. For a look at the visuals and code structure behind this tech, see this comprehensive rep counter guide.

To build a high-performance tracker, we must understand why using just one sensor usually fails during a workout.

  • : This sensor measures proper acceleration, including gravity. While it is stable over long periods, it is incredibly sensitive to vibrations and sudden shakes.
  • : This measures how fast a device rotates. It is excellent for tracking quick changes but suffers from "drift," where small errors accumulate until the orientation is completely wrong.

By fusing these two together, we get the short-term precision of the gyroscope and the long-term stability of the accelerometer.

The Logic of the Complementary Filter

A is a lightweight way to merge these signals without the high processing power required by complex math like Kalman filters.

The formula is straightforward: FusedAngle = α * (Gyro Angle) + (1 - α) * (Accel Angle). Usually, we set alpha to , meaning we trust the gyroscope for 98% of the data but use the accelerometer to "pull" the signal back to reality.

Building a Smart State Machine

Once we have a clean signal, we don't just count every "peak." Instead, we use a to track the phases of an exercise like a bicep curl.

  1. : The user is at rest.
  2. : The angle crosses a specific entry threshold (e.g., 30 degrees).
  3. : The user reaches the peak and begins the return movement.
  4. : Once the return is complete, the count increments and resets to IDLE.

This multi-step logic ensures that small jitters or mid-rep pauses don't lead to false counts, making the app feel much more professional and "intelligent."

Accuracy in Every Movement

This framework is highly adaptable to various exercises beyond the bicep curl. For , you can track the torso's pitch angle, while rely on the vertical movement and thigh orientation.

The demand for real-time, accurate health feedback is growing rapidly. Mastering sensor fusion allows you to build tools that users can actually trust for their daily progress. To see the Python implementation and start your own project, read WellAlly’s full guide.

items.title: 🛡️ Build a Smart Excel Data Cleaner in Python (Step-by-Step)
items.author: Mate Technologies
items.content:
A beginner-friendly walkthrough of SmartExcelGuardian – a professional Excel data cleaning and reporting tool built with Python.

A desktop GUI app for Excel cleanup

Automatic missing value handling

Heuristic “data health” scoring

Export results to Excel, PDF, JSON, and TXT

pip install pandas numpy openpyxl ttkbootstrap reportlab

📁 Project Structure SmartExcelGuardian/ │── logo.ico

1️⃣ Import Required Libraries

We start by importing everything we need.

import os, sys, threading, json
import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import filedialog
import ttkbootstrap as tb
from ttkbootstrap.constants import *
from datetime import datetime

ttkbootstrap → modern dark UI

threading → keep UI responsive during cleanup

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
from openpyxl import Workbook
from openpyxl.styles import PatternFill, Font
from openpyxl.utils.dataframe import dataframe_to_rows

Excel export & formatting (openpyxl)

Column name normalization (re)

from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import A4
from reportlab.lib.units import mm
from reportlab.lib.colors import red, orange, green, black

Used for generating professional PDF reports.

2️⃣ Global State & Logging

stop_event = threading.Event()
cleanup_results = {}
log_file = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "excelguardian.log")

stop_event → allows canceling cleanup

cleanup_results → shared export data

log_file → error tracking

3️⃣ Utility Helper Functions Resource Loader (for packaged apps)

def resource_path(file_name):
    base_path = getattr(sys, "_MEIPASS", os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    return os.path.join(base_path, file_name)

This helps when bundling with PyInstaller.

def clean_column_name(name):
    name = name.strip().lower()
    name = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", "", name)
    name = re.sub(r"\s+", "_", name)
    return name

Original Cleaned Total Sales ($) total_sales

def convert_numpy(obj):
    if isinstance(obj, np.integer): return int(obj)
    if isinstance(obj, np.floating): return float(obj)
    if isinstance(obj, np.ndarray): return obj.tolist()
    raise TypeError

Needed because JSON can’t serialize NumPy types.

4️⃣ Creating the Main Window

app = tb.Window(themename="darkly")
app.title("SmartExcelGuardian v1.1.0")
app.geometry("1100x650")

def heuristic_score(missing, duplicates, type_issue):
    score = 0
    score += min(30, missing * 2)
    score += min(30, duplicates * 2)
    score += 40 if type_issue else 0
    return min(score, 100)

Score Meaning Score Risk 31–70 Moderate 🟠 🔟 Data Cleaning Engine

def assess_and_clean(df):
    for col in df.columns:
        series = df[col]


coerced = pd.to_numeric(series, errors="coerce")
cleaned_series = coerced.fillna(coerced.mean())

✔ Converts to strings ✔ Fills missing values with most common value

items.title: **Navigating the Complexities of Managing a Minor's Trust: A Guide for Guardians and Relatives**
items.author: Insights YRS
items.content:
Navigating the Complexities of Managing a Minor's Trust: A Guide for Guardians and Relatives

As a guardian or relative entrusted with managing a minor's trust, you may face a multitude of challenges, including navigating the complexities of family dynamics and making informed investment decisions. In this article, we will delve into the intricacies of managing a minor's trust, providing guidance on how to balance the needs of the beneficiary with the concerns of interfering relatives.

Understanding the Role of a Trust Guardian

As the guardian of an $80,000 trust for a 15-year-old relative, you have a significant responsibility to manage the funds in a way that benefits the beneficiary's long-term financial well-being. This includes making investment decisions, paying taxes, and ensuring that the trust is administered in accordance with the terms of the trust agreement.

Dealing with Interfering Relatives

It's not uncommon for relatives to express concerns or offer unsolicited advice when it comes to managing a minor's trust. While their intentions may be well-meaning, their interference can create tension and undermine your ability to make informed decisions. To address this issue, consider the following strategies:

  1. Establish clear communication channels: Set clear boundaries and expectations with relatives regarding your role as trust guardian and the decision-making process. This will help prevent misunderstandings and ensure that everyone is on the same page.
  2. : Keep relatives informed about the trust's performance, investment decisions, and any significant events that may impact the beneficiary's financial situation.
  3. : If relatives continue to express concerns or offer unsolicited advice, consider seeking guidance from a financial advisor or attorney who can provide expert advice and help mediate any disputes.
  4. Focus on the beneficiary's best interests: Remember that your primary responsibility is to act in the best interests of the beneficiary. Make decisions that align with their long-term financial goals and well-being, rather than trying to appease relatives.

Teaching Good Money Habits

In addition to managing the trust, you may also want to teach your young relative good money habits and financial literacy skills. This can be a valuable opportunity to instill a strong understanding of personal finance and help them develop healthy financial habits.

  1. Encourage financial education: Consider enrolling your relative in a financial education program or course that teaches basic money management skills, such as budgeting, saving, and investing.
  2. Model good financial behavior: Demonstrate responsible financial habits yourself, such as saving, investing, and avoiding debt.
  3. Involve the beneficiary in decision-making: As the beneficiary grows older, involve them in the decision-making process, allowing them to take ownership of their financial decisions and develop a sense of responsibility.

Managing a minor's trust can be a complex and challenging task, particularly when dealing with interfering relatives. By establishing clear communication channels, providing regular updates, seeking professional advice, and focusing on the beneficiary's best interests, you can navigate these challenges and make informed investment decisions that benefit the beneficiary's long-term financial well-being. Additionally, by teaching good money habits and financial literacy skills, you can help your young relative develop a strong understanding of personal finance and set them up for long-term financial success.

items.title: **Title:** BlackRock's Stellar Performance in 2023: What Can Investors Expect in 2026?
items.author: Insights YRS
items.content:
BlackRock's Stellar Performance in 2023: What Can Investors Expect in 2026?

BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, has consistently demonstrated its resilience and adaptability in the ever-changing landscape of the global financial markets. The company's recent earnings report has provided valuable insights into its performance in 2023, and we will delve into the key highlights and implications for investors. As we look ahead to 2026, it is essential to understand the factors that contributed to BlackRock's success and how they may shape the company's future prospects.

A Year of Exceptional Growth

BlackRock's 2023 earnings report has been hailed as one of the strongest years in the company's history. The asset manager's net income rose by 15% year-over-year, driven by a combination of factors, including:

  1. Increased Assets Under Management (AUM): BlackRock's AUM grew by 12% in 2023, reaching a record high of $9.5 trillion. This significant increase in assets has provided the company with a substantial source of revenue.
  2. Strong Investment Performance: BlackRock's investment products have delivered impressive returns, with many funds outperforming their benchmarks. This has helped to attract new investors and retain existing ones.
  3. Diversification and Expansion: The company has continued to expand its product offerings and geographic presence, solidifying its position as a global leader in the asset management industry.

Key Takeaways from the Earnings Report

The earnings report has provided several key takeaways that are relevant to investors:

  1. : BlackRock's revenue grew by 14% year-over-year, driven by the increase in AUM and strong investment performance.
  2. : The company's operating expenses rose by 10% year-over-year, primarily due to increased investment in technology and talent.
  3. : BlackRock's ROE remained stable at 24.5%, indicating the company's ability to generate strong returns for its shareholders.

What Can Investors Expect in 2026?

As we look ahead to 2026, investors can expect several factors to shape BlackRock's performance:

  1. : The company's strong track record of growth is expected to continue, driven by the increasing demand for asset management services.
  2. : BlackRock is likely to continue investing in technology and expanding its product offerings to stay ahead of the competition.
  3. : The company will need to navigate the evolving regulatory landscape, which may impact its business operations and profitability.

BlackRock's stellar performance in 2023 has provided valuable insights into the company's strengths and weaknesses. As we look ahead to 2026, investors can expect the company to continue growing and innovating, driven by its strong track record and commitment to excellence. By understanding the factors that contributed to BlackRock's success and how they may shape the company's future prospects, investors can make informed decisions about their investments in the asset management industry.

items.title: **Revolutionizing AI-Powered Drug Development: The Rise of Chai Discovery**
items.author: Insights YRS
items.content:
Revolutionizing AI-Powered Drug Development: The Rise of Chai Discovery

The intersection of artificial intelligence (AI) and pharmaceuticals has given birth to a new era of innovative drug development. Among the pioneers in this space is Chai Discovery, a startup that has been making waves with its cutting-edge AI technology and strategic partnerships. In this article, we will delve into the journey of Chai Discovery, from its humble beginnings in OpenAI's offices to its recent deal with Eli Lilly, one of the world's leading pharmaceutical companies.

Chai Discovery's story began in the offices of OpenAI, a renowned AI research organization. The startup's founders, who were part of OpenAI's team, recognized the vast potential of AI in transforming the pharmaceutical industry. They saw an opportunity to leverage AI's capabilities in drug discovery, a process that has traditionally relied on trial and error. With a shared vision, the team set out to create a platform that could accelerate the discovery of new medicines using AI.

Chai Discovery's growth was fueled by strategic partnerships and investments from some of Silicon Valley's most influential venture capital firms. These partnerships not only provided the necessary funding but also brought valuable expertise and connections to the table. The startup's ability to attract top talent and secure significant investments has been a testament to its innovative approach and potential for impact.

One of the most significant milestones in Chai Discovery's journey was its partnership with Eli Lilly, a global leader in pharmaceutical research and development. This collaboration marked a significant step forward in the adoption of AI-powered drug development. By combining Chai Discovery's AI technology with Eli Lilly's expertise in pharmaceutical research, the two companies aim to accelerate the discovery of new medicines and improve patient outcomes.

Chai Discovery's rise to prominence in the AI-powered drug development space is a testament to the power of innovation and collaboration. From its humble beginnings in OpenAI's offices to its recent deal with Eli Lilly, the startup has demonstrated its potential to transform the pharmaceutical industry. As the field of AI-powered drug development continues to evolve, Chai Discovery is poised to play a leading role in shaping the future of medicine.

  • Chai Discovery's AI technology has the potential to revolutionize the pharmaceutical industry.
  • The startup's partnerships with influential VCs and Eli Lilly have provided significant funding and expertise.
  • Chai Discovery's collaboration with Eli Lilly marks a significant step forward in the adoption of AI-powered drug development.

As the healthcare industry continues to grapple with the challenges of developing new medicines, Chai Discovery's AI-powered platform is poised to make a significant impact. With its innovative approach and strategic partnerships, the startup is well-positioned to drive the next wave of innovation in pharmaceutical research and development.

items.title: How I Built a Daily Streak Keeper with Python & GitHub Actions
items.author: Rodney Gitonga
items.content:
A guide on automating GitHub activity to keep your contribution streak alive using Python and GitHub Actions.

🚀 How I Built a Daily Streak Keeper with Python & GitHub Actions

We've all been there—staring at that one gray square in our GitHub contribution graph that broke a perfect streak. Whether you're busy, sick, or just forgot to push code, losing a streak can be demotivating.

I decided to solve this problem by automating a daily commit using Python and GitHub Actions. Here is how I built my Daily Streak Keeper.

  1. Fetch a daily quote (to make the commit meaningful).
  2. Update the README.md with the new quote.
  3. Commit and push the changes automatically every day.
  • Python: To fetch the quote and update the file.
  • GitHub Actions: To schedule the script to run daily.
  1. The Python Script ()

I wrote a simple Python script that fetches a random quote from an API and updates the file. I used for stability.

  1. The Automation ()

Next, I needed this script to run automatically. GitHub Actions is perfect for this. I set up a workflow that runs every day at 12:00 PM Nairobi Time (09:00 UTC).

This was a fun little project to ensure my profile stays active while also giving me a nice quote to read every day. It’s a great example of how powerful and easy GitHub Actions can be for automating simple tasks.

Check out the code in the repository and feel free to fork it to create your own streak keeper! REPO

Goals: As extracted from the 'Python for Software Development' textbook by Halvorsen (n.d.):

  • Creating Functions in Python - Introduction
  • Functions with multiple return values
  • Creating Classes in Python

Notes: Python for Data Science, AI & Development Course (IBM) (Santarcangelo, n.d.):

Module 3: Python Programming Fundamentals

While doing the Hands-on-Lab for the topic on Loops (Santarcangelo, n.d.),

After inputting this code: dates = [1997,2001,20003]

for i in N: print(dates[1])

TypeError Traceback (most recent call last) Cell In[8], line 4

1 dates = [1997,2001,20003]

2 N = len(dates) 5 print(dates[1])

TypeError: 'int' object is not iterable

So a question came to mind: 'Why are integer objects in python not iterable?' I found this:

Why integer objects in python are not iterable (Ramakrishna, 2021):

  • Contain singular integer values
  • Do not have '' method

print(dir(int)) shows if an object contains '' method or not (Ramakrishna, 2021).

Summary: I learnt why integer objects in python are not iterable.
